description_long: This fire hydrant features a robust cylindrical body painted in a light cream color, with a domed cap and flanged base in a muted blue-green shade. The hydrant has two prominent side outlets capped with hexagonal nuts, and a central front-facing outlet, all of which are designed for hose attachment. The top of the hydrant is fitted with a large operating nut, used to open or close the water valve. Embossed manufacturer markings and technical details are visible on the rear side of the body. The surface shows signs of weathering and use, with some discoloration and minor surface imperfections. The overall construction is solid and industrial, typical of municipal fire hydrants found in urban environments.
description: A sturdy, cream-colored fire hydrant with a blue-green domed cap, three hose outlets, and visible manufacturer markings. The surface is weathered and industrial, with hexagonal caps and a prominent operating nut on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet, two side outlets, and the operating nut on top; the front outlet is most prominent.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting one side outlet and the cylindrical body, with the cap and base clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the embossed manufacturer markings and both side outlets; the central outlet is not visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite side outlet and the cylindrical body, with the cap and base in profile.
